events.title: Breathwork + Soundbath
events.description:
A practice of Holotropic Breathwork, Meditation and Sound Bath to quiet the mind, open the heart & connect with ourselves & each other on a deeper level.

Breathwork is a healing practice and experience that we can all benefit from. Whether you are looking to reconnect to more joy & peace, de-stress from your work week, or to strengthen your connection to your intuition and creativity. The session will include a guided meditation and intention setting, introduction to holotropic breathwork and what to expect, a guided breathwork session followed by relaxing sound bath.

\*What to wear: comfortable clothing, long sleeves/layers encouraged

\* If you are pregnant or if you have epilepsy, please consult your primary care physician to see if breathwork is right for you before practicing.

Some benefits of breathwork include:

- Connect to your creativity

- Increase Self Love and appreciation

- Stress relief

- Increased Self Awareness

- Ease symptoms of Depression

- Ease symptoms of Anxiety

- Peace of mind

- Open the heart

- Increased Intuition

- Relaxation

- Release trauma and fear

- Move stuck energy and assist in processing emotions and feelings

10-Class Pass Rate: $11

Drop-In Rate: $22...

events.description:
"I Swear" Screens Friday Jun. 5 and Saturday Jun. 6 at 7:00 PM and Sunday Jun. 7 at 6:00 PM at the Jim Santy Auditorium.

Based on the true story, "I Swear" is a moving, uplifting account of the life of John Davidson, a Tourette syndrome campaigner who grew up with the condition in 1980s Scotland, at a time when it was little known and misunderstood. Featuring outstanding performances from Robert Aramayo, Peter Mullan, Shirley Henderson and Maxine Peake, "I Swear" is a frank, funny an d touching story about the transformative power of friendship and community.

Tickets and more information at http://parkcityfilm.org...

events.title: La Milpa Planting Starts - June 8th
events.description:
**La Milpa Planting Starts | June 8, 2026 | 5:00 - 7:00 pm**

Together, we will gather as a community for La Milpa Planting of the Starts (baby plants) in the Community Garden. With our climate, we must plant these delicate, young plants later in the season. We acknowledge ancestral and current Indigenous Maya & Mesoamerican practices as we give gratitude to the land and plant culturally-relevant crops that complement La Milpa. This gathering will be in Spanish and English. A sample of delicious, traditional food will be offered. Registration is required for this free event. Doors open at 5 p.m.; program begins at 5:30.

**Comienza la siembra de La Milpa | 8 de junio de 2026 | 17:00 - 19:00 h**

Nos reuniremos como comunidad para la siembra de plántulas (plantas jóvenes) en el huerto comunitario de La Milpa. Debido a nuestro clima, debemos plantar estas delicadas plantas jóvenes más adelante en la temporada. Reconocemos las prácticas ancestrales y actuales de los pueblos indígenas mayas y mesoamericanos al dar gracias a la tierra y plantar cultivos culturalmente relevantes que complementan La Milpa. Esta reunión se llevará a cabo en español e inglés. Se ofrecerá una degustación de deliciosa comida tradicional. Es necesario inscribirse para este evento gratuito. Las puertas se abren a las 5:00 p. m.; el programa comienza a las 5:30 p. m....

events.title: Park City Film Presents: "American Pachuco: The Legend of Luis Valdez"
events.description:
"American Pachuco: The Legend of Luis Valdez" Screens Thursday Jun. 11 at 7:00 PM

An auteur emerges from America's underclass: from migrant farmworker to revolutionary artist, Luis Valdez changed American culture. In the 1960s, his El Teatro Campesino performed on flatbed trucks and helped mobilize workers to win the first farmworker union contract. His "Zoot Suit" was the first Chicano play on Broadway. Despite critical rejection that killed the show, he persevered, creating "La Bamba"-a breakout blockbuster that authentically depicted Mexican-American life to the world. Now in its 60th year, El Teatro continues to be a beacon for Latino/a creators. This is the story of an artist who gave voice to the overlooked and opened pathways for generations.

Winner of the 2026 Sundance Film Festival Audience Award for U.S. Documentary and Audience Award for Festival Favorite.

Presented as part of Latino Arts Festival. In Spanish and English with English subtitles. Admission is free with registration.

Tickets and more information at http://parkcityfilm.org...

events.title: Park City Film Presents: NATCHEZ
events.description:
"Natchez" captures an unsettling clash between history and memory in a small Mississippi town; a layered mosaic of people contending with the weight of the past in a place where it is always present.

Equal parts amusing and disturbing, we journey through an antebellum tourist destination at a crossroads as it grapples with a deeply troubled history that is so thoroughly ingrained in its present, we're left to wonder if it's actually past at all.

"The genteel politeness on display at the start of the film falls away, revealing an unsettling core" -HOLLYWOOD REPORTER

Post-film Q&A with director Suzannah Herbert.

Part of the America the Beautiful Series \(June 26-28\) - a celebration of the diversity of the American Experience and the American people on the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence.

More information at http://parkcityfilm.org...

events.title: Park City Film Presents: Remaining Native
events.description:
Ku Stevens \(17\) is the solo runner at his high school with no coach. Living on the Yerington Paiute reservation in Northwest Nevada he needs more to be seen by his dream school, the University of Oregon. As Ku trains, unreconciled emotions unearth the memory of his great-grandfather, Frank Quinn. At 8 years old, Frank ran 50 miles across the desert to escape an Indian boarding school. Frank's story becomes interwoven withKu's journey to run a collegiate qualifying time. Will Ku outrun his history or will he learn to run in parallel with it to achieve his dreams?

Post-film Q&A with director Paige Bethmann.

Part of the America the Beautiful Series \(June 26-28\) - a celebration of the diversity of the American Experience and the American people on the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence.

Tickets and more information at parkcityfilm.org http://parkcityfilm.org/...

events.title: Jylian Gustlin + Lola: Exhibition
events.description:
Gallery MAR is pleased to present a summer exhibition featuring new work by Lola and Jylian Gustlin, opening **July 3rd** with an artist reception from **6–9 pm** at the gallery’s Main Street location in Park City. On view for **three weeks**, the exhibition brings together two distinctive contemporary artists whose practices explore abstraction through color, material, and perception.

Lola, a self-taught visual artist based in the San Francisco Bay Area, is best known for her intensely vibrant abstractions created with tinted epoxy resin. Working on a raised horizontal surface, she allows the resin to flow, pool, and spill over the edges of the panel, creating luminous compositions framed by gravity-defying drips that have become a signature of her work. Her newest series, *Quiet Places*, marks an evolution in her practice—moving away from densely layered forms toward simpler shapes, softer palettes, and shadowy overlays that invite viewers to experience moments of calm and serenity. For Lola, color remains the central language of emotion, and each work is intended to create an experience that allows viewers to find their own meaning and sense of optimism within the composition.

In conversation with Lola’s emotive abstractions are the richly layered paintings of Jylian Gustlin. Influenced by the San Francisco Bay Area Figurative painters and informed by her background in computer science and digital graphics programming, Gustlin’s work explores the intersection of art, mathematics, and perception. Drawing on concepts such as the Fibonacci sequence and entropy, she creates complex compositions using oil, acrylic, charcoal, wax, gold leaf, pastel, and graphite. Gustlin builds her surfaces through drawing, painting, and scratching into the material itself, resulting in paintings that feel almost sculptural—dynamic fields of gesture, symbol, and color that encourage viewers to look closely and discover new relationships within the work.

Together, Lola and Gustlin...
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Michael Kessler: All Resin
events.description:
Michael Kessler has spent decades exploring the boundary between gesture and geometry, and his resin works bring that inquiry into sharper focus than ever.

Each painting is built from up to fifty micro-thin translucent layers, fused and accumulated until something luminous emerges. Biomorphic forms weave through geometric structures, nature's logic made visible through an almost meditative process of application and restraint. Kessler likens his gestural freedom to a kind of painterly tai chi, controlled, but never rigid.

In his all-resin works, that layered depth takes on new dimension. Light travels through the surface rather than simply reflecting off it, making each piece feel as much like a living object as a painting.

Join us August 7th to experience new works by Michael Kessler at Gallery MAR, Park City....
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Matt Flint: Sleeping in the Forest
events.description:
Matt Flint doesn't begin with a plan. No sketches, no predetermined composition, just canvas, paint, and a process as intuitive as the landscapes that shaped him.

Raised in rural Missouri and now living in Wyoming's Wind River Mountains, Flint has spent a lifetime in close study of the natural world, its textures, its rhythms, its constant state of becoming. That attention finds its way into every surface he paints. His works build through layers of creation and destruction, each image emerging as another recedes, holding the tension between what was and what might come next.

The result is painting that moves the way wilderness does, familiar and elusive at once, inviting you closer while always suggesting there's something just beyond the next ridge.

Join us September 5th to experience new works by Matt Flint at Gallery MAR, Park City....
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Serve Park City: Park City's 3rd Annual 9/11 national Day of Service and Remembrance
events.description:
Save the Date for Serve Park City: Park City's 3rd Annual 9/11 National Day of Service and Remembrance. The day starts at 8 a.m. in City Park with a complimentary breakfast for all volunteers, followed by a short program that honors 9/11/01. At 9 a.m. all volunteers head to their Serve Park City service projects they have signed up for on JustServe.org/ServeParkCity that are being held on 9/19/26. (These projects will be posted by August 1, 2026) Last year we had 35 different projects all over the Park City area that ran from 9 a.m. -12 noon with over 1200 volunteers participating. We invite all to serve. 

This event is hosted by Serve Park City, Inc. A nonprofit whose mission is to build community through service. Each year we partner with over 30 nonprofits and organizations in hosting a day of service, but find opportunities to serve throughout the year on JustServe.org/ServeParkCity. Learn more about us at serveparkcity.org...

events.title: Last Friday Gallery Stroll
events.description:
Hosted by the Park City Gallery Association, the Last Friday Gallery Stroll, is a fun \(and free\) monthly community event dedicated to highlighting artists and special exhibits at art galleries throughout town. Park City's art scene continues to thrive, offering a robust variety of unique art collections from local, national, and international artists. On the last Friday of each month, seventeen of our art galleries open their doors for visitors and locals alike to enjoy light refreshments and to admire their latest art installations.

Park City Gallery Stroll runs from 6:00 - 9:00pm on the last Friday of each month. It's the perfect way to explore the Park City art scene while enjoying scenic Historic Main Street and our beautiful alpine evenings....

events.title: Savor the Summit
events.description: Dine al fresco on Park City's Historic Main Street — Deer Valley returns to Savor the Summit for an unforgettable evening of open-air dining on Park City's Historic Main Street. The evening begins with a social hour featuring champagne pours from Laurent-Perrier and gourmet appetizers. Then, gather around our signature eight-foot paella pan, featuring squid ink rice with lobster, razor clams, octopus, and shrimp, as Deer Valley’s culinary team prepares a three-course dinner before your eyes. Paired with select wines from Duckhorn Vineyards, this is an experience you won't want to miss. Limited seating available. Secure your spot today. View Menu Reserve Now
events.location: Historic Park City Main Street, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Guided Historical Hike
events.description: Discover Park City's mining history — Step into Park City’s past on a guided hike through Deer Valley’s scenic terrain, where fresh mountain air meets stories of the area’s rich mining history. This three- to four-hour hike covers moderate terrain, typically under six miles, with sweeping views around Little Baldy. Meet at Snow Park Lodge, by the Stein Eriksen statue, for an 8:30 a.m. departure.

events.title: Beethoven's Ninth
events.description: Presented by Deer Valley & Utah Symphony — If there’s one classical masterpiece everyone should experience live, it’s Beethoven’s Symphony No. 9. From its mysterious opening to its thrilling finale, the piece builds toward a powerful celebration of humanity and hope with the “Ode to Joy,” as the orchestra, chorus, and soloists unite in one of the most recognizable melodies ever written. Now imagine hearing it beneath the open skies and surrounded by the Wasatch Mountains, performed by the extraordinary musicians of the Utah Symphony, the Utah Symphony Chorus, and vocal soloists under the baton of our Music Director Designate Markus Poschner—an internationally acclaimed Beethoven interpreter, making his first appearance at our summer home. Beethoven’s Ninth is a monumental work and an unforgettable way to open our summer season. July 17 | Gates Open: 6:30 p.m. | Show Time: 8 p.m. July 18 | Gates Open: 6 p.m. | Show Time: 7:30 p.m.

events.title: Lyle Lovett
events.description:
Lyle Lovett is an americana singer-songwriter and actor from Klein, Texas, who has been described as "the thinking man's cowboy".  While typically associated with the alt-country genre, his albums typically incorporate folk, swing, blues, jazz and gospel music as well as more traditional country & western styles.

 

 The four-time Grammy winner has acted in a number of films and been a guest-star on television.  Lovett was married to actress Julia Roberts for a short time in the mid-1990s.  In 2002, he was trampled by a bull while working on his uncle's farm in Texas, but soon recovered and resumed touring....

events.title: Stravinsky’s Firebird Suite & String Trio “Time for Three”
events.description: Presented by Deer Valley & Utah Symphony — Stravinsky’s Firebird Suite takes flight in this spectacular program led by Utah Symphony Music Director Designate Markus Poschner. First, GRAMMY®-winning trio Time for Three—known for their genre-defying blend of classical, Americana, and contemporary styles— takes the stage for Kevin Puts’ Contact, the winner of a GRAMMY® for Best Contemporary Classical Composition. Written specifically for the group by the Pulitzer Prize-winning composer, the work explores themes of human connection and highlights the trio’s dynamic, longtime partnership with the Utah Symphony. Then, hear one of the most thrilling pieces ever written echoing through the Wasatch Mountains as the orchestra takes you on a journey from the explosive “Infernal Dance” to the powerful finale of Stravinsky’s Firebird. Gates Open: 6 p.m. | Show Time: 7:30 p.m.
events.location: Snow Park Outdoor Amphitheater, 2250 Deer Valley Dr S #501, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Reception & Five-Course Dinner
events.description: The Taste of Luxury Series featuring Stag's Leap — Deer Valley’s Taste of Luxury Series continues with a celebration honoring the 50th anniversary of the 1976 Judgment of Paris, the landmark tasting that forever changed the global perception of California wine and established Napa Valley as a world-class wine region. To commemorate the occasion, Deer Valley welcomes Marcus Notaro, head winemaker of Stag's Leap Wine Cellars, alongside returning guest Chef Clement Gelas of Talisker Club for an intimate evening at Deer Valley Café. Set pond-side at sunset, guests will enjoy a thoughtfully curated five-course pairing experience inspired by the wines, stories, and legacy behind this groundbreaking moment in wine history. Gozney’s professional-grade ovens, engineered to hit blazing temperatures over 900°F with their signature rolling flame, set the stage for Matilda’s wood-fired Neapolitan pizzas, crafted with the same passion that’s made them a local favorite among both travelers and regulars. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from Chef Peter Menteer’s showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Chef Ryan Arensdorf Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience Fire & Flavor Festival, where live-fire cooking meets alpine views. On August 1 and 2, Chef Ryan Arensdorf of KC Que takes the lead with Kansas City-style BBQ, featuring slow smoked meats, classic sides, and sweet cornbread. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, this weekend brings bold, fire-driven flavor to a relaxed mountain setting, where guests can gather and refuel between activities. Chef Peter Menteer prepares paella in an 8-foot pan with local meats and fresh seafood, alongside Chef Sam Phelan’s plank salmon cooked over open fire pits. Local Utah breweries and live entertainment at Silver Lake Village round out the experience. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.

events.title: Allen Stone
events.description:
Grammy-nominated artist Allen Stone delivers an unforgettable fusion of soul, funk, and feel-good energy. Allen Stone, a soul/R&B l uminary, has enraptured global audiences with his electrifying performances and soulful melodies, embodying the essence of classic soul legends.

His genre-defying style, blending funk, gospel, folk-rock, and so ul, has garnered critical acclaim and a devoted following. Stone's journey to musical stardom commenced in Chewelah, Washington, where his upbringing immersed him in gospel music as a pastor's son. Relocating to Seattle, he tirelessly toured the West Coast in his '87 Buick, swiftly gaining acclaim for his dynamic live shows, often playing up to 200 dates annually alongside luminaries such as Stevie Wonder, Al Green, Dave Matthews Band, Erykah Badu, and Gary Clark Jr. Notably, Stone recently hit  the road with Chris Stapleton, further solidifying his status as a tour de force in the music industry.

Alongside his prolific touring schedule, Stone has graced national television screens and mentored on ABC's American Idol, underscoring his esteemed position in the music realm. Stone continues to garner critical acclaim with his latest body of work, Mystery, featuring the title track in collaboration with PJ Morton. This release brings infectious rhythms and emotional depth, solidifying Stone's place as a powerful voice in modern soul music....

events.title: Boney James
events.description:
Saxophonist, Producer and Composer Boney James’  New Album ‘Slow Burn’
slow burn: noun, informal
A feeling that grows with a slow but deliberate intensity.
For saxophonist and composer Boney James, these words perfectly describe the music he has  crafted for his new Concord Records album, Slow Burn, to be released on October 18, 2024.
“When I mentioned that phrase to people as a possible title, they said, “That also refers to your  whole career in a way” James says with a laugh. “This is my 19th record and I feel like I’m just  hitting my stride.”
Slow Burn is a new high point in a remarkable career that now spans more than three decades  and includes a long list of accolades; four GRAMMY nominations, three NAACP Image Award  nominations, a Soul Train Award for Best Jazz Album and four RIAA Gold Record certifications.  In 2009, Billboard named James one of the Top 3 Contemporary Jazz Artists of the decade. In  2024 year Boney became the first ever artist to score 20 number one singles on the Billboard Smooth Jazz chart.
But Boney James has never been one to look backwards and Slow Burn, which the artist  produced, marks several firsts. Beginning with the album’s opening track, ‘Arcadia.’ The very first notes on the album are the acoustic bass playing of the legendary Marcus Miller.  It’s one of the few times that Miller, known for his electric bass guitar work, has committed his  standup bass playing to a recording and the first time that James has incorporated an upright bass  into his own music
Next is James’ intriguing interpretation of the classic ‘Butterfly.’ Co-written by master  keyboardist Herbie Hancock and Bennie Maupin, the track features Marcus Miller again as well  as young lion Cory Henry’s inspired electric piano. “This beautiful melody got stuck in my head”  explains James. “I have never covered a tune by a legend like Herbie before and I hope we did  him justice.”
The title track, ‘Slow Burn,’ is one of four tunes on the album that James co-wrote an

events.title: UB40 featuring Ali Campbell
events.description:
UB40 Featuring Ali Campbell – Live in Concert
Get ready for a legendary night of reggae under the stars as UB40 featuring Ali Campbell brings their iconic sound to Concerts on the Slopes in Park City, where Music Meets the Mountains.
With Ali Campbell’s unmistakable voice leading the way, UB40 has delivered some of the most beloved songs of all time, including Red Red Wine, Can’t Help Falling in Love, Kingston Town, Here I Am (Come and Take Me), and Food for Thought. Their music has defined generations, blending reggae rhythms with pop sensibility and global appeal.
Set against the stunning backdrop of Canyons Village at Park City Mountain, Concerts on the Slopes offers a one-of-a-kind outdoor concert experience. Enjoy cool mountain temperatures, sweeping alpine views, and an energetic yet intimate setting with 5,000 seats and top-tier sound and production.
Make it a full Park City experience—arrive early, explore the village, and settle in for an unforgettable summer evening filled with timeless music, mountain air, and good vibes.

events.title: Better Than Ezra with Special Guests Vertical Horizon and TONIC
events.description:
Get ready for a night of iconic ’90s and early-2000s alternative rock as Better Than Ezra and Vertical Horizon take the stage together for an unforgettable live show.
Better Than Ezra, known for their chart-topping hits like “Good,” “Desperately Wanting,” and “King of New Orleans,” rose to prominence during the alternative rock boom of the 1990s. With their melodic hooks, thoughtful lyrics, and energetic live performances, the band has remained a fan favorite for decades.
Joining them is Vertical Horizon, the multi-platinum band behind massive radio hits “Everything You Want,” “You’re a God,” and “Best I Ever Had.” Their emotionally driven songwriting and polished rock sound helped define an era of modern alternative and continue to resonate with audiences today.
Together, these two beloved bands deliver a hit-packed night of nostalgia, sing-along anthems, and timeless alternative rock. Whether you grew up with these songs or discovered them later, this show promises a high-energy, feel-good concert experience you won’t want to miss.
Music has an undeniable way of bringing people together, creating connections that run as deep as family. That bond is at the heart of Tonic, the GRAMMY® Award–nominated, multi-platinum rock trio made up of Emerson Hart (vocals, guitar), Jeff Russo (lead guitar), and Dan Lavery (bass).
Since forming in the mid-1990s, Tonic has built a lasting legacy through eloquent songwriting, lyrical honesty, and unforgettable melodies. Their debut album Lemon Parade went platinum and produced the iconic “If You Could Only See,” the #1 most-played rock song of 1997. The band went on to score six Top 10 singles, sell over 4 million records, and earn two GRAMMY® Award nominations, including for “Take Me As I Am.”
More than two decades later, Tonic’s music continues to resonate across generations. Fans—young and old—still sing every word, proving the songs are bigger than time and bigger than the band itself. After brief breaks and successful solo caree

events.title: Maoli
events.description:
Now breaking into the mainstream with a one-of-a-kind blend of island reggae and country soul, Hawaiian-born rising star Maoli is bringing his people—and his sound—to the world like never before.
The golden-voiced native of upcountry Maui has released nine independent albums, earned over half a billion global streams, and spent nearly 15 years touring and recording, becoming a major fixture across the Pacific Islands and beyond. Inspired by traditional Hawaiian music (the birthplace of the steel guitar), reggae, rock, and country legends like Dolly Parton, Willie Nelson, and George Strait, Maoli has built a signature style that connects Polynesia to the American West Coast—and all the way to Texas.
With a growing global fanbase, Maoli’s Hawaiian-country sound has fueled viral hits and powerful covers, including Brett Young’s “Mercy” (18+ million Spotify streams) and “In Case You Didn’t Know” (10+ million Spotify streams). Now teaming up with hit producers Dann and David Huff, Maoli continues bridging the gap between island soul and country tradition with his latest release, Last Sip of Summer (Island Vibe Edition)—out now.

events.title: Rick Springfield
events.description:
RICK SPRINGFIELD BIO: 
Over the past four decades, Rick Springfield has worn many hats as an entertainer and performer. The creator of some of the finest power-pop of the ’80s, a Grammy winning singer, songwriter, and musician who has sold 25 million albums and scored 17 U.S. Top 40 hits, including “Jessie’s Girl,” “Don’t Talk to Strangers,” “An Affair of the Heart,” "I've Done Everything for You,” “Love Somebody,” and “Human Touch.” He’s an accomplished actor who has starred opposite Meryl Streep in the feature film “Ricki and the Flash,” gave a chameleonic performance as the creepy Dr. Irving Pitlor in HBO’s prestige drama “True Detective,” earned great reviews for his portrayal of Lucifer on the CW hit “Supernatural” and most recently played the insane Pastor Charles on FX’s worldwide hit series “American Horror Story.” An author as well, both his candid 2010 memoir Late, Late at Night (which Rolling Stone named one of the 25 greatest rock memoirs of all time) and his 2014 comedic novel Magnificent Vibration earned rave reviews and spots on the New York Times Best Sellers’ list. In 2014, Springfield was honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, located around the corner from the first apartment he lived in when he first arrived in the U.S. from Australia in 1971. Springfield’s Sirius-XM radio show, Working Class DJ, is a huge hit and airs weekly on 80s on 8. His new album “Automatic” is out now. Automatic is his first album of all new original material in 5 years.

events.title: Dylan Scott
events.description: ABOUT DYLAN SCOTT:Despite a multi-Platinum story and six No. 1 singles, including two fresh smashes in 2025 alone, Curb Records hitmaker Dylan Scott has always kept an underdog mindset. A rowdy roughneck with a romantic side – one whose modern edge was sharpened by timeless, emotional twang – the consummate family man broke out with the epic triple-Platinum devotion of “My Girl” in 2016 and followed up with scrappy, under-the-radar consistency. Chart-topping heart-forward anthems like “Hooked,” “Nobody” and “New Truck” all reached RIAA-certified status, but last year launched the crooner into the stratosphere. Posting back-to-back No. 1s with Gold-certified “Boys Back Home” (with Dylan Marlowe) and Platinum-certified “This Town’s Been Too Good to Us,” Scott wrapped 2025 as the third most-played country artist of 2025 and continues his hot streak with his current single, Platinum-certified “What He’ll Never Have,” which nears the Top 10 at Country radio and was honored by ASCAP as one of the most-performed country songs of 2025. With nearly 5 billion career streams, legions of fans and countless sold-out shows to his credit, his stardom has somehow grown in relative stealth. But with his third full album, Easy Does It, he’s not sweating. Sure of himself, his talent and his fans, Scott heads toward a new creative chapter with total confidence. Featuring 11 tracks, his signature vocal and vivid intensity take center stage on a set defined by personal truth – whether through heartbreak, romantic devotion or small-town dedication. The in-demand live act spent last year on his Country Till I Die Tour, headlining his biggest venues yet including his first-ever arenas and record-breaking attendance, and his Easy Does It Tour. He will continue headlining across the country on his Till I Can’t I Will Tour, launching this spring.

events.title: 80s Weekend with Taylor Dayne and Exposé
events.description:
Taylor Dayne
Taylor Dayne is an American pop icon. With a career spanning three decades, Taylor’s Groundbreaking debut single “Tell It To My Heart” turned her into an overnight international star in 1987. She followed the smash hit with seventeen Top 20 singles over the course of her three-decade career, including Number Ones “Love Will Lead You Back”, “Prove Your Love”, and “I’ll Always Love You.”
Taylor has sold over 75 million albums and singles worldwide, earned three GRAMMY nominations, an American Music Award, multiple New York Music Awards, received New York Music Hall of Fame honors and ranked as the Number 18 Female Dance Artist of all time by Rolling Stone magazine. She is one of the very few artists in musical history to successfully crossover into almost every musical genre and chart with hit singles in Pop, Dance, R&B, Adult contemporary, and Rock.
Exposé
Get ready for a night of unforgettable 80s and 90s dance-pop when Exposé takes the stage. This legendary female vocal group from Miami helped define the freestyle and dance-pop sound of the late 1980s and early 1990s, delivering a string of iconic hits that still fill dance floors today.
With powerhouse vocals and timeless melodies, Exposé became the first group ever to score four Top 10 hits from their debut album, including classics like “Come Go With Me,” “Point of No Return,” “Let Me Be the One,” and their No. 1 smash hit “Seasons Change.”
Featuring the celebrated voices of Jeanette Jurado, Ann Curless, and Gioia Bruno, Exposé continues to tour and delight fans worldwide with their signature blend of high-energy dance anthems and heartfelt ballads.
From chart-topping radio hits to beloved freestyle club favorites, Exposé delivers a show packed with nostalgia, powerful vocals, and infectious rhythms that will have audiences singing along all night.
Join us for an electrifying evening celebrating one of the most successful girl groups of the 80s and a sound that defined a generation.
events.location: 4000 Canyons Resort Drive, Park City, UT

events.title: Country Weekend - Mark Chesnutt
events.description: Mark Chesnutt is one of country music’s most iconic voices, known for his unmistakable honky-tonk sound and a catalog of timeless hits including “Too Cold at Home,” “Brother Jukebox,” and “I Don’t Want to Miss a Thing.” A defining artist of the ’90s country era, Chesnutt earned 14 No. 1 singles, 23 Top 10 hits, over 12 million albums sold, and the CMA Horizon Award, with Billboard naming him one of the most-played country artists of the decade. Now energized and fully focused on performing, Chesnutt continues to deliver powerful, authentic live shows that celebrate classic country at its finest.
events.location: 4000 Canyons Resort Drive, Park City, UT

events.title: Country Weekend - Lonestar
events.description:
Lonestar is a celebrated American country music group that earned global recognition with their timeless ballad “Amazed,” a crossover sensation that dominated the charts for 9 weeks at No. 1 on the country charts and 2 weeks at No. 1 on the pop charts. Known for blending heartfelt storytelling with a polished pop-country sound, the band — comprised of Dean Sams, Michael Britt, Keech Rainwater, and Drew Womack — has made a lasting impact on the genre.
With an impressive 22 top ten singles on the Billboard Hot Country Songs chart, including 10 that reached No. 1, Lonestar has built one of the most successful track records in modern country music. Their rise began in 1995 with the top-five single “Tequila Talking,” followed by chart-topping hits such as the rock-tinged “No News,” “Come Cryin’ to Me,” and “Everything’s Changed.”
The release of their quadruple-platinum 1999 album Lonely Grill firmly established their legacy, producing four No. 1 singles and showcasing the band’s signature ability to craft emotionally resonant, radio-friendly songs.
Lonestar’s achievements have earned them widespread acclaim, including multiple Academy of Country Music awards for New Vocal Group, Single of the Year, Song of the Year, and Humanitarian of the Year. They have also been honored by the Country Music Association with Vocal Group of the Year and the International Artist Achievement Award.
With more than 10.5 million records sold worldwide, Lonestar remains one of country music’s most enduring and influential bands, continuing to inspire fans with their heartfelt hits and remarkable musical legacy.
events.location: 4000 Canyons Resort Drive, Park City, UT

events.title: Country Weekend - Walker Hayes
events.description:
Country superstar Walker Hayes is a singer-songwriter from Mobile, Alabama, known for blending country storytelling with pop and hip-hop-inspired production. His breakout smash “Fancy Like” topped country sales and streaming charts, spent over six months at No. 1 on Billboard’s Hot Country Songs chart, reached the Top 5 on the Billboard Hot 100, hit No. 1 at country radio, and earned Hayes his first GRAMMY nomination for Best Country Song. The viral hit was also featured in a nationwide Applebee’s commercial.
With additional fan favorites like “AA,” “Y’all Life,” and “U Gurl,” Hayes has become one of the top-selling and most-streamed artists in modern country music. His latest project, 17 Problems, is his most personal work to date—an introspective album shaped by resilience, faith, family, grief, and growth. Featuring both high-energy anthems and deeply emotional storytelling, the album highlights Hayes’ evolution as an artist and songwriter.
Known for his authenticity and engaging live performances, Hayes has appeared on Good Morning America, The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon, Late Night with Seth Meyers, TODAY, and CBS Sunday Morning. Whether delivering chart-topping hits or stripped-down acoustic moments, Walker Hayes invites audiences into a powerful, honest, and unforgettable live experience.
events.location: 4000 Canyons Resort Drive, Park City, UT

events.title: Marc E. Bassy with MAX

Multi-platinum artist MAX leads the night with his powerhouse vocals, infectious pop sound, and global hits like “Lights Down Low” and music from his latest album, LOVE IN STEREO, which have earned over 3 billion streams worldwide. Known for his dynamic stage presence and emotional delivery, MAX brings a live show full of energy, connection, and sing-along moments.
Special Guest - OpenerJoining him is Bay Area artist Marc E. Bassy, celebrated for his soulful vocals, genre-defying style, and hit “You & Me” (feat. G-Eazy). With a career built on authenticity and creative independence, Marc delivers a performance that feels both intimate and electrifying.Together, they bring powerful vocals, emotional storytelling, and undeniable chemistry to the stage—creating a dynamic live experience you won’t want to miss.

events.title: Old Town walking tour
events.description: Step beyond Main Street and experience Park City with a local! This guided tour blends mountain-town charm, historic buildings, Olympic legacy, art-filled tunnels, and more! Gain an insider perspective through a small-group setting designed to help you feel truly connected to Park City. We'll meet upstairs inside Old Town Transit Center (easily accessed by Park City's fare-free bus!) to wander for 1-1.5 hours and just under a mile. Weather-appropriate layers and a water bottle are recommended, families are encouraged, and gratuities are appreciated. Use code PR20 for 20% off!
events.location: Silver Skis, 558 Swede Alley, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Purposeful Volunteering: Find Your Fit, Build Impact
events.description:
Discover how to align your passions with meaningful service in this engaging panel discussion—moderated by **Suzy Berg Lyon**, featuring panelists **Karen Scheible**, **Scott Beck**, and **Amy Macuga**—on purposeful volunteering. Whether you’re new to giving back or looking to deepen your impact, this session will explore how to find the right opportunities, stay motivated, and make a lasting difference rooted in real needs in your community.

Join us for an inspiring conversation filled with practical insights, personal stories, and actionable guidance to help you volunteer with intention and impact.

Interested in joining? Contact alexis@parkcitycf.org...

events.title: (Re)sounding: Art Exhibition
events.description: Sound is not simply something we hear, but something we inhabit, shape, and are shaped by. Through immersive audio installations, evocative visual translations of sound, and interactive artworks that invite participation, the artists in (Re)sounding explore sound as a dynamic subject and material. This exhibition is an invitation to listen more deeply—to feel the weight and resonance of sound, to dwell inside it, and to hear the world in new ways.

events.title: Pro Active Alliance Monthly Speaker Series
events.description:
**June 3 - Wasatch Immigration Project**

We are honored to welcome Maggie AbuHaider, co-founder of the Wasatch Immigration Project, as our featured speaker and presenter. After serving in judicial clerkships and as a Civil Assistant United States Attorney handling complex civil defense and immigration appeals, Maggie has dedicated her legal career to helping individuals navigate the challenges of the U.S. immigration system. In 2023, she co-founded the Wasatch Immigration Project to provide compassionate legal support for asylum seekers and immigrant youth. Maggie will share insights into today’s immigration landscape, the real-life impact on families and communities, and the important work being done locally through the Wasatch Immigration Project.

The event will be held at the Kiln Coworking Space in Newpark, 5:30-7:30. We hope to see you there! Please RSVP to let us know you are coming. (801)673-7642...

events.title: Medical Care in Park City in 1900-Not For the Faint of Heart lecture given by Dr. Thomas Schwenk
events.description:
Park City Museum will host a lecture called Medical Care in Park City in 1900—Not For the Faint of Heart given by Dr. Thomas Schwenk on Wednesday, June 3, 2026 from 5 to 6 p.m. at the Park City Museum Education and Collections Center located at 2079 Sidewinder Drive, Park City UT.  

Life was rough and death common in the remote mining town of Park City in the late 1800s and early 1900s. This presentation will discuss common diseases and injuries of the era, the doctors and nurses who cared for the town, and the funding and building of the much-needed Miners Hospital in 1904. 

Dr. Thomas Schwenk is a board-certified family physician who practiced in Park City from 1978 to 1984, the first full-time physician in town following the closure of the mines. A graduate of the University of Michigan Medical School, he also trained at the University of Utah. Following his academic career at the University of Michigan and the University of Nevada, Reno, he returned to Park City with his wife, Jane. They have children in Denver and Brooklyn, both of whom were born in Utah and consider themselves Parkites....
events.location: Education and Collections Center, 2079 Sidewinder Dr, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.description: Public Meetings & Listen Live Historic Preservation Board Meetings - How to Watch, Listen, and Submit Public Comment Virtually: Listen | Watch | Submit Comment:  LINK \(Watch and Listen Live link is activated at the start of the meeting\) \*Advance online registration required. events.title: painting with wool
events.description: painting with wool Thursday, June 4th | 6 PM | Our Lady of the Snows | $15 | Sign-up Required In this class, students will learn how to needle felt using a single felting needle and a variety of colorful wool fibers to create a 2D wool painting. Wool painting is done by pushing wool fibers into a flat piece of wool felt with a foam pad underneath. Students will learn how to blend wool colors and a number of techniques for forming desired shapes and textures with the wool. If you enjoy creating with repetitive motion and a medium that is easy to adjust and learn with, this class is for you! Students will leave with a 6x6 inch landscape wool painting of Mount Wolverine. This event is for 10 and up. Amanda has lived in and out of Utah for 35 years and now resides in Sandy with her family. Always a lover of art and the outdoors, she spent many hours as a child in the woods behind her home drawing, painting, and creating. Amanda has dabbled in a wide variety of artistic mediums, always drawn to realism, nature, the stories of people, and the details of ordinary life. She discovered needle felting during a challenging season of my life. She found it a therapeutic and forgiving medium - easy to experiment with and providing many possibilities for color and texture. Amanda works to capture the wonder of creation and the beauty of ordinary things in our local environment through wool painting. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility....

If you’ve been thinking about getting out, reconnecting, and doing something that actually fills your tank… this is it. This is a \*\*fully covered retreat\*\* — all you need to do is get yourself to Salt Lake City. We handle the rest: • Lodging • Food • Motorcycle • Guided rides through incredible terrain No stress. events.title: Park City Film Presents: I Swear
events.description:
"I Swear" Screens Friday Jun. 5 and Saturday Jun. 6 at 7:00 PM and Sunday Jun. 7 at 6:00 PM at the Jim Santy Auditorium.

Based on the true story, "I Swear" is a moving, uplifting account of the life of John Davidson, a Tourette syndrome campaigner who grew up with the condition in 1980s Scotland, at a time when it was little known and misunderstood. Featuring outstanding performances from Robert Aramayo, Peter Mullan, Shirley Henderson and Maxine Peake, "I Swear" is a frank, funny an d touching story about the transformative power of friendship and community.

Tickets and more information at http://parkcityfilm.org...

events.title: Modern Calligraphy for Beginners
events.description: Join us at Mountain Town Olive Oil in Park City, Utah for pretty lettering! Whether you're a stationery nerd, DIY Bride-to-be, or just a crafty individual, you'll be introduced to modern calligraphy with a pointed dip pen and ink in this workshop. We will cover all the basics and prepare you to add those unique lettered details to your snail mail, wedding paper goods, home decor, and more! The perfect crafty afternoon out! Class includes 1.5 hours of instruction and demonstration, our beginner's calligraphy kit \(two nibs, black ink pot, 2 letter guides, tracing paper, and straight pen holder\), and all materials to write on. Everything is yours to keep at the end of the workshop! Check out their website and say hey on Instagram to learn more about Mountain Town Olive Oil Co. Get your tickets below! Feel free to come early, grab your seat and mingle with friends. Class will start promptly at 1 PM! Class Expectations: Yes, lefties are welcome too! This class is encouraged for someone who has never taken a calligraphy class before. We teach the basics, and our expectation for you is to have ink flowing through your nib with enough confidence to be able to practice way beyond class. We love using tracing paper with our guides to help you build muscle memory and develop your own style! If you have tried calligraphy out before, this class will be a great refresher and hopefully a chance to try some new supplies from our beginner kit that will re-introduce you to this lovely form of art! A note on bringing guests 16 and under: We love to share this passion with kids. We ask that a ticketed adult accompany anyone under the age of 16 at all times. To see our cancellation/refund policy, please click here....
events.location: Mountain Town Olive Oil Co, Park City, UT

events.title: Brush Lettering for Beginners at Mountain Town Olive Oil Co
events.description: Join us atMountain Town Olive Oil Coin Park City, Utah for a workshop of pretty brush lettering! If you've always admired beautiful stationery, handwritten signage used at weddings or in home decor, or just love all hand-lettered crafts, this workshop will leave you feeling confident implementing this art form into your events or everyday life! Class includes 1.5 hours of instruction and demonstration, our beginner's brush lettering kit \(tracing paper, our Brush Lettering Guides Bundle, a large Brush Marker, a small black Fudenosuke marker\), and all materials to write on. Everything is yours to keep at the end of the class! Check out their website and say hey on Instagram to learn more about Mountain Town Olive Oil Co. Get your tickets below! Feel free to come early, grab your seat and mingle with friends. Class will start promptly at 4 PM! Class Expectations: Yes, lefties are welcome too! events.title: National Trails Day
events.description: Join the Mountain Trails Crew and dig with us on June 6th to celebrate National Trails Day at Sparky Trailhead:  https://maps.app.goo.gl/fHc4zosLDCzMy2Fr5  In celebration of National Trails Day, Mountain Trails Foundation will be performing maintenance on the small, but beloved Clark Ranch system in Richardson Flat.  The anticipated projects will include weed removal along the trail\(s\) corridor, hand-building additional beginner features on Lower Sparky’s, trimming back encroaching vegetation, and hand-rebuilding berms on Upper Sparky’s. Please bring enough food and water for the duration of the event. Also, if you are able to bring your own work gloves, long-sleeve shirt and sunglasses, that would be appreciated. Work tools and instruction will be provided.  Minors are welcome but must attend with a guardian.  To make sure we can safely accommodate volunteers, this is an RSVP-only event. Please be sure to mark "Going" to this Facebook event so that we can accommodate you. Please consider others and cancel your sign-up if you cannot make it! The schedule for the day will be as follows: 8:00 - 8:30am  - Meet up and check-in. Enjoy bagels from Wasatch Bagel and Coffee from Silver King 8:30 – 9:00am – Safety talk and hike to work site 9:00 - 12:00pm – Trail work 12:30pm – Lunch Waivers are required every year for each participant and must be filled out beforehand: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdGpEehj4t8X4AkxwyseMgjIzErC6p2WDGm1lWhIxUdKM6DiQ/viewform?vc=0&c=0&w=1&flr=0 For your comfort and safety, please come prepared with the following: Water Sun Protection Gloves Snacks Closed-Toe Footwear If you have any questions, please contact Ian Hartley : Ian@mountaintrails.org...

The Park Silly Sunday Market mission is: 

 An ecofriendly open air market, street festival and commUnity forum where neighbors and friends come together to celebrate our commUnity of fun and funky Park Sillians.

The Vision Of The Park Silly Sunday Market Is:

To grow and expand the experience of the inclusive quality of commUnity. To create worldwide recognition of Park City, Utah as the place where local, national and international causes are brought to light and addressed in the spirit of celebration and connected

more info: 

https://parksillysundaymarket.com...
events.location: Park Silly Sunday Market, Park City, UT

events.title: English-Spanish Conversation Club | Club de conversación español-inglés
events.description:
Practice your Spanish and English conversation skills in a group setting at Park City Library and learn how to have realistic everyday conversations. For people ages 14 and up of all language skill levels: beginners welcome! Visit parkcitylibrary.org for more information.

Venga a Park City Library a practicar sus habilidades de conversación en inglés y español, y aprenda a llevar conversaciones cotidianas de forma natural. Todos son bienvenidos, aun si no hablen mucho inglés. Para adultos y adolescentes mayores de 14 años. Visite parkcitylibrary.org para aprender más.
events.location: Park City Library, Park City, UT

events.title: Next Chapter Book Club
events.description: Join Park City Library's book club for adults with varying developmental abilities. Eat lunch, read books, and share stories with friends. For more information, contact Rylee Broach: rylee.broach@parkcity.org or 435-615-5602.
events.location: Park City Library, Park City, UT

events.title: Interactive Mixology Class
events.description:
Learn to mix, shake, and enjoy your own handcrafted, seaso nally inspired cocktails! Upon arrival, we will greet you with a glass of sparkling rosé or brut. Sit back and sip while your instructor explains the bartender's tools of the trade and how to use them. Then, get your cameraphone ready, as you will be expertly guided through two shaken-or-stirred recipes to ensure a delicious final product.

Perfect for:

  \* Bachelor/ Bachelorette Parties

  \* Birthday & Anniversary Celebrations

  \* Honeymoons

  \* Team-Building Events and Corporate Retreats

Choose your theme!

  \* Seasonally-Inspired/Holidays

  \* Farm-to-Table Margaritas or Smashes

  \* Old Fashioned versus Manhattan

  \* Sparkling Wine Cocktails

  \* New Orleans Favorites

  \* NA and Mocktail Classes

Must be 21 to enter Old Town Cellars. No guests under 21 allowed....
events.location: Old Town Cellars, Park City, UT

events.title: Community Day
events.description: Kick off the summer with a discounted Gold Pass to the Utah Olympic Park on June 8, 2026. We love our local community and want to extend our appreciation with $49 Gold Passes for the full day. Book your tickets early before we sell out! Come enjoy the Extreme Zip, Extreme Tubing, Alpine Slide, Freestyle Zip, Action Tower, and Discovery Course from 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM.
events.location: Utah Olympic Park, Park City, UT

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description:
Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date.

A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere.

Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Mozart, Prokofiev, & More
events.description: Mozart's gripping Overture to Don Giovanni opens the concert with ominous chords and cleverly placed silence, building tension before racing forward with exhilarating momentum. Prokofiev&\#03's sparkling Sinfonietta follows, bursting with playful rhythms colored by the composer&\#03's youthful wit and charm. The program concludes with Louise Farrenc's Symphony No. 3, an expressive work admired in her lifetime by musicians including Schumann and Berlioz for its elegant structure and dramatic range, moving between brooding drama and romantic warmth.
events.location: St. Mary's Catholic Church, 1505 White Pine Canyon Rd, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Haydn, Mendelssohn, & A Viola Concerto
events.description: The rich voice of the viola takes center stage as Utah Symphony Principal Viola Brant Bayless performs Dai Fujikura’s Wayfinder, a concerto inspired by Polynesian navigation traditions. In this imaginative work, the orchestra becomes the natural forces used to find direction—stars, winds, and ocean swells—while the soloist listens and charts a path forward through shifting musical currents. Like a musical guide through the evening, Maurice Cohn conducts a program that unfolds across vivid sonic landscapes, beginning with Mendelssohn’s atmospheric The Hebrides, which evokes Scotland’s rugged coastline with dark, rolling lines in the low strings set against shimmering winds and strings that suggest the sea air. The journey concludes with Haydn’s jubilant Symphony No. 104—his final symphony—whose finale bursts with rustic, folk-inspired energy that resonates beautifully in the intimate acoustics of St. Mary’s Catholic Church....
events.location: St. Mary's Catholic Church, 1505 White Pine Canyon Rd, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Beethoven's Ninth
events.description: If there’s one classical masterpiece everyone should experience live, it’s Beethoven’s Symphony No. 9. From its mysterious opening to its thrilling finale, the piece builds toward a powerful celebration of humanity and hope with the “Ode to Joy,” as the orchestra, chorus, and soloists unite in one of the most recognizable melodies ever written. Now imagine hearing it beneath the open skies and surrounded by the Wasatch Mountains, performed by the extraordinary musicians of the Utah Symphony, the Utah Symphony Chorus, and vocal soloists under the baton of our Music Director Designate Markus Poschner—an internationally acclaimed Beethoven interpreter, making his first appearance at our summer home. Beethoven’s Ninth is a monumental work and an unforgettable way to open our summer season....
events.location: Snow Park Outdoor Amphitheater, 2250 Deer Valley Dr S #501, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Mozart’s “Jupiter”
events.description: Utah Symphony Music Director Designate Markus Poschner leads an all-Mozart program starring Concertmaster Madeline Adkins in Mozart’s second violin concerto. Written when he was just 18, the concerto showcases effortless virtuosity with the elegance and clarity that would continue to define his voice throughout his career. Framing the concerto are Mozart’s two final symphonies—written just weeks apart—revealing the fascinating journey of the composer's musical maturation and his unmistakable brilliance. One of Mozart’s most recognizable works, his Symphony No. 40 uses a minor key—a rare choice for the composer—to build emotional intensity and dark lyricism. In contrast, the program concludes with the joyful Symphony No. 41, whose grandeur and musical tour de force ultimately earned it the nickname “Jupiter,” honoring the Roman god....
events.location: St. Mary's Catholic Church, 1505 White Pine Canyon Rd, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Beethoven’s Symphony No. 7
events.description: Promising a night of lyrical beauty and orchestral drama, conductor Louis Lohraseb shapes a program that follows an arc of adoration to heartbreak in the first half. The season finale at St. Mary’s Catholic Church opens with Wagner’s tender Siegfried Idyll, a deeply personal expression of love written as a birthday gift for his wife. Then the spotlight turns to Erica Petrocelli in two dramatic works that showcase the brilliance and expressive power of the soprano voice: Mozart’s virtuosic “Come scoglio” from Così fan tutte, a declaration of unwavering fidelity, and Mendelsohn's “Infelice,” where sweet memories offer fleeting comfort after a lover’s betrayal. Famously called “the apotheosis of the dance itself” by Wagner, Beethoven’s Symphony No. 7 brings the night to a triumphant close. An instant symphonic favorite—with early audiences reportedly demanding a repeat of the second movement—the piece surges with wildness and joy, culminating in one of the most electrifying finales in classical music....
events.location: St. Mary's Catholic Church, 1505 White Pine Canyon Rd, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Park City Trail Series 5K
events.description: Fill your summer calendar with trail races! Park City Trail Series is a 3-race series that runs from June – August. It starts with a 5K and works up to a half marathon. The courses are forgiving enough for new trail runners but bring the excitement that seasoned trail runners love. You can mix and match which races you would like to sign up for or get all three at an amazing price! Plus, you can 10% per person by signing up 4 or more people for the series. So, get your family, friends, neighbors, or whoever you need to recruit, and get signed up today! THERE ARE NO DEFERRALS OR REFUNDS.
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: RVR 1/2 Marathon & 7K Trail Run
events.description: The Round Valley Rambler offers two distances, 7K or a trail half marathon. The half marathon distance is the first race in the Triple Trail Challenge.    100% of the course takes place on buff single and double track trails within the 2,100 acre, Round Valley Open Space, with altitude ranging from 6,500 to 7,100 feet. While athletes will find the trails foot-friendly, the rolling, high-desert terrain presents a few long and steady climbs followed by fast downhills.
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: Moose on the Loose Kid #1
events.description: Join us for Moose on the Loose 2026, a four-race trail running series in Park City for kids ages 3–14. Built to be fun, welcoming, and confidence-building, the series gives young runners a chance to race on local trails, stay active, and enjoy a supportive community atmosphere. Recommended race distances are available by age to help athletes find the right fit. The 2026 race dates are June 13, July 18, August 15, and September 12, with all races beginning at 9:00 AM. This year’s race venues include RTS Trailhead, Round Valley North, Quinn's Trailhead, and Utah Olympic Park, offering a variety of exciting trail settings throughout the summer. Athletes may register for individual races at $25 each or sign up for the full four-race series and save $5 per race, for a total savings of $20. We believe kids can do hard things while having fun. Our races are designed to be achievable and enjoyable for all levels of runners, with the goal of inspiring kids to do their best, push themselves, and believe in themselves....
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: Women's Epic 10K at Deer Valley
events.description: Women's Epic Race at Deer Valley Resort is a 10k loop with a 1335ft vertical trek to the peak and back down. You can hike, jog, or run your journey through gorgeous trees with changing fall leaves and steep climbs. You will undoubtedly come off the mountain with more confidence and grit after completing this race to the top of the world-class Deer Valley Resort located in Park City Utah. And when you get to the finish line you can grab a drink and celebrate!

You are not allowed to bring alcohol on the train! And, when the train returns you can stick around for a live concert featuring The Flashback Brothers. events.title: Midway Swiss Days
events.description: The annual Swiss Days Festival takes place in Midway, Utah, the Friday and Saturday before Labor Day each year and draws a huge crowd both days. Swiss Days includes a 10K race, parade, live entertainment, and so much more. DATES: Friday, September 4 & Saturday, September 5, 2026 SCHEDULE: Friday, Sep 4th: Market 8:00 am - 8:00 pm Saturday, Sep 5th: Market 8:00 am - 8:00 pm Breakfast & 10K Run start at 7:00 am, Parade at 10:00 am PARKING: Two public parking locations offer a free shuttle bus to and from the event location. Parking is $10 and the shuttle runs approximately every 15 minutes. There is limited street parking and private pay parking lots surrounding the square.‍ Complimentary bike parking is available at Centennial Park. PETS: Pets are NOT allowed at the venue.‍

The concert is family-friendly, free to attend, and open to everyone—bring a friend and join us at the library amphitheater during the summer months or indoors during winter and inclement weather for a vibrant night of music and community!

events.title: Dominick Antonelli @ Cafe Galleria
events.description: Live Summer Music Series May through September (weather permitting) Every day but Mondays from 5:30pm – 8:30pm Sundays 10am – 1:30pm Dominick has been playing guitar for 21 years and writing original music for 15+ of those years. He looks at himself not as a guitar player, but an artist who utilizes the guitar and other forms to create an inspiring, healing, and contemplative experience for himself and listeners. Alternate tunings and intuitive playing have contributed immensely by shaping the instrumental and lyrical music that comes through him. He has a background in Integrative Health and is currently studying Music and Recording Arts Technology which is taking his creative possibilities to new depths and heights. He has performed at festivals in Thailand, Costa Rica, and the East Coast (US), as well as in yoga classes, local gigs, and workshops where he incorporates live music, meditation, group energy healing and writing into one experience. He loves performing live and sharing the gifts that continue to be cultivated. Read more about Dominick
events.location: Cafe Galleria, 101 West Main Street

events.title: Wine Tasting %26 Cheese Train
events.description: Step aboard for a relaxed and thoughtfully curated Wine Tasting Experience on the train, where scenic rail travel meets a guided exploration of classic wines from around the world - sponsored by Vine Lore. Guests will enjoy a five-course wine tasting, each pour selected to showcase a range of styles and regions. The experience is designed to unfold at an easy, enjoyable pace, encouraging conversation, discovery, and time to savor each wine. Each table will be presented with a generous shared cheese board, available throughout the journey for guests to enjoy alongside the wines at their leisure. You'll ride in one of our beautiful vintage lounge cars. Tickets are sold either by the half table for 2 people (may be shared with 2 other guests), a full table for 4, and a limited number of single seats. Wines on the menu: Decoy Brut Cuvee Kumusha Sauvignon Blanc Cest La Vie Rouge (Pinot Noir & Syrah Blend) Larchago Tempranillo - Rioja, Spain Graham's Six Grapes Ruby Port
events.location: Heber Valley Railroad, 450 S 6th W, Heber City, UT 84032

It will be an evening filled with music that celebrates America. 📅 Saturday, July 4, 2026 🕗 8:00 PM 📍 Midway Town Square 🎟 Free to attend Bring your blankets, chairs and your patriotic spirit-this is a night you won’t want to miss!

events.title: Board & Donor Speaker Series: Purposeful Volunteering: Find Your Fit, Build Impact
events.description: Discover how to align your passions with meaningful service in this engaging panel discussion—moderated by Suzy Berg Lyon , featuring panelists Karen Scheible , Scott Beck , and Amy Macuga —on purposeful volunteering. Whether you’re new to giving back or looking to deepen your impact, this session will explore how to find the right opportunities, stay motivated, and make a lasting difference rooted in real needs in your community. Join us for an inspiring conversation filled with practical insights, personal stories, and actionable guidance to help you volunteer with intention and impact.
events.location: Blair Education Center, Park City Hospital, 900 Round Valley Dr, Park City, UT

events.title: Pro Active Alliance Monthly Speaker Series
events.description: June 3 – Wasatch Immigration Project We are honored to welcome Maggie AbuHaider, co-founder of the Wasatch Immigration Project, as our featured speaker and presenter. After serving in judicial clerkships and as a Civil Assistant United States Attorney handling complex civil defense and immigration appeals, Maggie has dedicated her legal career to helping individuals navigate the challenges of the U.S. immigration system. In 2023, she co-founded the Wasatch Immigration Project to provide compassionate legal support for asylum seekers and immigrant youth. Maggie will share insights into today’s immigration landscape, the real-life impact on families and communities, and the important work being done locally through the Wasatch Immigration Project. The event will be held at the Kiln Coworking Space in Newpark, 5:30-7:30. We hope to see you there! Please RSVP to let us know you are coming....
events.location: Kiln Park City, 1090 Center Dr., Park City

events.title: Family Friendly: Wind Chimes in Clay
events.description: Create a one-of-a-kind ceramic wind chime for your home or garden. Using basic hand-building techniques, you’ll shape, texture, and decorate your clay pieces to form the chime’s body. We’ll guide you through the process, from forming the pieces to attaching the chimes for beautiful sound. You’ll learn creative glazing techniques to give your wind chime a vibrant, lasting finish. No prior experience is necessary—just bring your creativity! The price is per project, and we welcome kids under 12 when accompanied by an adult.
events.location: Kimball Art Center, 1251 Kearns Blvd, Park City, UT

events.description: Summit County’s Stormwater Coalition will celebrate National Trails Day (June 6) with the return of Trails, Trash and Tunes. events.title: Demonstration Garden Volunteer
events.description: Help bees, butterflies, and other pollinators by assisting with our demonstration garden in front of the EcoCenter! Volunteers will help with soil amendment, weeding, and pruning the pollinator garden in front of the EcoCenter to maintain it for the growing season. This opportunity is a great way to learn about native and waterwise plants that do well in Park City. Volunteers will receive instruction and guidance upon arrival from gardening professional Colie Belieu ( yogaearthdesign.com ). Dress for the weather and for gardening. We’ll provide the tools and supplies. Everyone is welcome, from beginners to Utah Master Gardeners! Can’t stay for the whole time? No problem! Join for as long as you are able....

events.title: Summit County Dems Fundraiser
events.description: Wednesday, June 24 6:00 to 8:00 PM The Red Banjo 322 Main Street, Park City Featuring Special Guest Brian King, Chair of the Utah Democratic Party We’re launching this fundraising campaign with a fun, informal evening featuring community conversation, food, and an opportunity to help build a stronger, more effective local political organization rooted in competence, integrity, and community-focused leadership. Tickets are $100 and include buffet pizza and a drink ticket. We also offer a no-questions-asked sliding scale because we believe civic participation should be accessible to everyone. Your support will directly fund voter outreach efforts here in Summit County at a time when outside political spending and coordinated campaigning are expected to play a larger role in local races. If you cannot attend, we hope you will still consider making a contribution in support of this work!...

events.title: Live PC Give PC
events.description: Live PC Give PC will take place on FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 6, 2026! HELP SHAPE PARK CITY’S FUTURE IN A SINGLE DAY. Every November, Live PC Give PC brings our community together through LivePCGivePC.org for 24 hours of extreme generosity. Children break open their piggy banks. Young people dip into their savings. Businesses kick in matching grants. With everyone in the community giving whatever they can, we’ve raised millions of dollars for local nonprofits. It’s how we’re working together to preserve the place we love—so it will continue to thrive for generations to come.

events.title: American Indian Services Native Market Days
events.description: Native Market Days | July 10–11, 2026 Canyons Village | Park City, Utah Native Market Days is a signature cultural experience hosted by American Indian Services, celebrating Native art, culture, fashion, dance, storytelling, and community. Held at Canyons Village in Park City, this event brings together Native artists, performers, designers, families, community members, and visitors in a vibrant setting that honors creativity, connection, and cultural expression. This two-day event will feature a Native Market, hoop dance performances, an art competition and judging experience, community gatherings, and a Native Fashion Show. Sponsors and partners are invited to help elevate Native voices, support Native artists and entrepreneurs, and align their brand with a meaningful cultural experience rooted in respect, representation, and celebration. events.title: River in Reverse
events.description: Artists Terry Chambers, Bland Hoke, Jr., and Shane Lindsey. “River in Reverse” depicts 16 miles of the Snake River, from Dead Man’s Bar to the Jackson Hole Airport. The work is backlit at night with color-changing LED lights to reflect the changing color of the river throughout the seasons. “River in Reverse” is a byproduct of a commission for the Jackson Hole Airport, using the scrap metal that would have otherwise been recycled. Installed in September 2011, “River in Reverse” wraps around The Center’s ceramics studio along the highly-trafficked pathway connecting Glenwood and Cache. The sculpture and its site are perfectly suited to each other: the Snake’s predetermined course magically flows around the building’s features – a window, a stairwell. During the day, light skates across the brushed steel. At night, LED lights mounted underneath the steel beneath plexiglass transform the river into a nocturnal experience like no other in Jackson.
events.location: Center for the Arts, 240 S Glenwood St, Jackson, WY, 83001
events.source: Center for the Arts Jackson Hole

events.title: Prismatic Menagerie
events.description: Prismatic Menagerie Origami-inspired sculptures made by Hacer Opening June 1st, 2024 We are adding a bold pop of color to the Cache Street corridor this summer! Take a wander on the wild side and explore the Prismatic Menagerie from the National Museum of Wildlife Art to the Center for the Arts—it’s a straight shot down Cache Street/Hwy 89. With seven bold origami-inspired sculptures, this exciting outdoor exhibition brings Hacer’s work to Jackson through a collaboration between two of Jackson’s impactful art institutions. His installation at the Museum includes six sculptures (a bear, a rabbit, a coyote, and three bison). The exhibition at The Center will host Bixby, a magenta elephant located on the Cache Street Sculpture Pad. The artist known as Hacer, which in Spanish is the verb meaning “to make,” creates sculptures from steel, which he appears to seamlessly fold into various abstractions and animal forms. His animals, including bears, rabbits, and an even more fantastical Pegasus, take their shape from origami, an artform that originated in East Asia, which Hacer became fascinated with in childhood. He was seven years old and living in a foster home when he first heard the story of Sadako and the Thousand Paper Cranes, by Eleanor Coerr. His determination to become a sculptor and develop his technique came after seeing the innovative sculptures of the well-known artist Alexander Calder. Hacer learned firsthand about experimenting with form and color while fabricating works for Jeff Koons and Ellsworth Kelly.
events.location: Center for the Arts, 240 S Glenwood St, Jackson, WY, 83001
events.source: Center for the Arts Jackson Hole

events.title: Patrick Dougherty: Finders Keepers
events.description: Patrick Dougherty is an American environmental artist, best known for his sculptures and installations built with sticks and saplings. Finders Keepers, a site-specific sculpture, was constructed over the course of three weeks with the help of many local volunteers. It is made entirely out of locally sourced saplings found in Teton and Sublette Counties. This sculpture is temporary and we hope all ages will enjoy it throughout the seasons for as long as possible. For your safety, and to help ensure others can enjoy this artwork over time, please do not climb on the sculpture. You are welcome to explore, wander, and enjoy! About Patrick Dougherty: Born in Oklahoma in 1945, Patrick Dougherty grew up in North Carolina, where he roamed the woods as a child. In the early 1980’s, he combined his carpentry skill with a love of nature and began to build sculptures using tree saplings as construction material. His work quickly evolved from single pieces on conventional pedestals to monumental-scale environmental works, which required saplings by the truckloads. He has built over 320 of these massive works, and become internationally acclaimed. His sculpture has been seen worldwide — from Scotland to Japan to Australia, and all over the United States. Finders Keepers is generously sponsored by: Agnes Bourne, CLB Architects, Engelhard Foundation, Charles and Lisa Claudy Fleischman Family Fund, Astrid Flood, Pamela Gibson, Maggie and Jim Hunt, Moorehead Family Fund, Anne and David Palmer, Shari Brownfield Fine Art, and Karen Stewart
events.location: Center for the Arts, 240 S Glenwood St, Jackson, WY, 83001
events.source: Center for the Arts Jackson Hole

events.description: Two unique music education nonprofits present Music Together programming for youngsters aged 0-7 four days a week at The Center! Join a class on Mondays and Thursdays with MusicLand or Wednesdays and Saturdays with Teton Music School! MusicLand MusicLand offers a comprehensive range of music education programs for children of all ages. From our Music Together program classes for ages 0-5, where families can explore music making together, to their classical violin & cello instruction utilizing the Suzuki method, and after school youth orchestra programmer grades 7-12. Teton Music School TMS unlocks a life of music for children and adults – whether you just want to jam along to your favorite playlist or perform at the top of your game, TMS can help you get there. events.description: Center for the Arts is proud to host two invaluable music education nonprofits on its campus. MusicLand MusicLand offers a comprehensive range of music education programs for children of all ages. events.title: Visual Arts Classes
events.description: As a resident organization on The Center’s campus, the Art Association of Jackson Hole (A!A) hosts programming across five studios, an art supply store, and an art gallery. A!A provides over 10,000 square-feet of space for community members to learn, practice and exhibit art. Offering Jackson Hole art classes and workshops to adults and kids of all levels, A!A instructs on painting, drawing, ceramics, sculpture, photography, silversmithing, glass, printmaking, mixed media, and more. It also hosts outreach sessions for more than 30 service organizations in the valley, providing underserved groups – including low-income or at-risk youth – with access to the arts. The Art Association produces several popular annual events, including Art Fair Jackson Hole, Arts on the Green, Teton Mudpots, Whodunnit? Anonymous Art Sale & Show, and The Holiday Bazaar. To learn more about the Art Association, visit their website for a full list of programming for children and adults.
